onlineFindings.name: TheAlgorithms
onlineFindings.usefulness: coverage map, folder conventions, self-checking examples, contribution/test discipline
onlineFindings.licenseBoundary: do not bulk-copy code; individual repositories use different licenses, including GPLv3 and MIT

onlineFindings.name: TheAlgorithms repositories
onlineFindings.usefulness: language-by-language repo structure and algorithm naming crosswalk
onlineFindings.licenseBoundary: treat as reference inventory unless a per-file license review allows reuse

onlineFindings.name: Rosetta Code Data Project
onlineFindings.usefulness: language/task coverage discovery and idiom comparison
onlineFindings.licenseBoundary: Rosetta content is documentation-style licensed; use as inspiration/research, not copied implementation source

onlineFindings.name: awesome-transpilers / jarble transpiler note
onlineFindings.usefulness: evidence that subset JS/TS translation exists, but only for simple programs
onlineFindings.licenseBoundary: use as a pointer to architecture patterns; production correctness still needs our own emitters/tests

byArchetype.label: Suffix arrays / suffix trees / text indexes

recommendedPolicy: Do not copy external implementation source into this repo without per-file license review.
Use public algorithm repositories for coverage maps, naming crosswalks, and test ideas.
Prefer canonical contracts plus emitters over JS-to-everything transliteration.
Count only cells whose generated/native source passes local tests as verified-native.
Treat unusual targets such as SQL, Solidity, Move, WAT, CUDA, Verilog, and assembly as specialist lanes.
